title: Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Haryana

overview.short: Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary is situated in Panchkula district of Haryana state, India.
overview.description: Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary is situated in Panchkula district of Haryana state, India. It is spread over an area of 767.30 hectares (1,896.0 acres). It also houses Vulture Conservation and Breeding Centre, Pinjore.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jatayu Conservation Breeding Centre, Pinjore
topPlaces.description: The Jatayu and Sparrow Conservation Breeding Centre (JCBC) is the world's largest facility for the breeding and conservation of Indian vultures and the house sparrow (Passer domesticus). It is located within the Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary in the town of Pinjore in the State of Haryana, India.

topPlaces.name: Himshikha
topPlaces.description: Himshikha is a planned, small township located near Pinjore in district Panchkula, Haryana, India, on the Pinjore—Mallah Road. The township was built by the Haryana Housing Board between 1985 and 1989.

topPlaces.name: Kaushalya Dam
topPlaces.description: The Kaushalya Dam (Hindi: कौशल्या बांध) is an earth-fill embankment dam on the Kaushalya river, which is a tributary of Ghaggar-Hakra River (modern remnant of ancient Sarasvati river), in Pinjore of Haryana state, India. It was constructed between 2008 and 2012 with the primary purpose of water supply.

topPlaces.name: Pinjore
topPlaces.description: Pinjore is a town in Panchkula district in the Indian state of Haryana. This residential 'township', located close to Panchkula, Chandigarh and Solan, is set over 1,800 feet above the sea level in a valley, overlooking the Sivalik Hills.

topPlaces.name: Yadavindra Gardens
topPlaces.description: Yadavindra Gardens, also known as Pinjore Gardens, is a historic 17th century garden located in Pinjore city of Panchkula district in the Indian state of Haryana. Panchkula city is nearby it.

topPlaces.name: Bhima Devi Temple Site Museum
topPlaces.description: The Bhima Devi Temple Complex, nicknamed Khajuraho of North India for its erotic sculptures, comprises the restored ruins of an ancient Hindu temple dating from between 8th and 11th century AD, together with the adjacent 17th-century Pinjore gardens (a variant of Mughal gardens), located in Pinjore town in Panchkula district of the state of Haryana, India. The old temple was destroyed by Islamic invaders and the present 8-11th CE temple is likely built on the same place under the old name, and the nearby ancient baoli still has old Hindu pillars.

topPlaces.name: Khol Hi-Raitan Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Khol Hi-Raitan Wildlife Sanctuary is situated in Panchkula district of Haryana State, India. It is 0.5 kilometres (0.31 mi) away from Panchkula on the Morni Road and its aerial distance from the Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary is only 3 kilometres (1.9 mi).

topPlaces.name: Chandi Mandir
topPlaces.description: Chandi Mandir (Hindi, Mandir: "Temple") is a Hindu temple dedicated to Chandi the goddess of power, near Chandigarh, located on NH-5 Chandigarh-Kalka highway in Panchkula city of Haryana state of India. It is about 15 km from the city of Chandigarh, which was named after the temple, and about 10 km away from the Mansa Devi Shrine.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Jatayu Conservation Breeding Centre, Pinjore & around

items.note: The Jatayu and Sparrow Conservation Breeding Centre (JCBC) is the world's largest facility for the breeding and conservation of Indian vultures and the house sparrow (Passer domesticus). It is located within the Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary in the town of Pinjore in the State of Haryana, India.

Himshikha is a planned, small township located near Pinjore in district Panchkula, Haryana, India, on the Pinjore—Mallah Road. The township was built by the Haryana Housing Board between 1985 and 1989.

The Kaushalya Dam (Hindi: कौशल्या बांध) is an earth-fill embankment dam on the Kaushalya river, which is a tributary of Ghaggar-Hakra River (modern remnant of ancient Sarasvati river), in Pinjore of Haryana state, India. It was constructed between 2008 and 2012 with the primary purpose of water supply.

Pinjore is a town in Panchkula district in the Indian state of Haryana. This residential 'township', located close to Panchkula, Chandigarh and Solan, is set over 1,800 feet above the sea level in a valley, overlooking the Sivalik Hills.
Yadavindra Gardens, also known as Pinjore Gardens, is a historic 17th century garden located in Pinjore city of Panchkula district in the Indian state of Haryana. Panchkula city is nearby it.

The Bhima Devi Temple Complex, nicknamed Khajuraho of North India for its erotic sculptures, comprises the restored ruins of an ancient Hindu temple dating from between 8th and 11th century AD, together with the adjacent 17th-century Pinjore gardens (a variant of Mughal gardens), located in Pinjore town in Panchkula district of the state of Haryana, India. The old temple was destroyed by Islamic invaders and the present 8-11th CE temple is likely built on the same place under the old name, and the nearby ancient baoli still has old Hindu pillars.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Khol Hi-Raitan Wildlife Sanctuary & around

items.note: Khol Hi-Raitan Wildlife Sanctuary is situated in Panchkula district of Haryana State, India. It is 0.5 kilometres (0.31 mi) away from Panchkula on the Morni Road and its aerial distance from the Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary is only 3 kilometres (1.9 mi).

Chandi Mandir (Hindi, Mandir: "Temple") is a Hindu temple dedicated to Chandi the goddess of power, near Chandigarh, located on NH-5 Chandigarh-Kalka highway in Panchkula city of Haryana state of India. It is about 15 km from the city of Chandigarh, which was named after the temple, and about 10 km away from the Mansa Devi Shrine.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bir Shikargah Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jatayu Conservation Breeding Centre, Pinjore, Himshikha, Kaushalya Dam, Pinjore, Yadavindra Gardens.
